error.signature: Vercel serverless function times out at 10s even though code completes in time locally
error.regex: (FUNCTION_INVOCATION_TIMEOUT|Vercel.*timeout.*10s|serverless.*function.*timeout|504.*Vercel.*gateway|Vercel\ serverless\ function\ times\ out\ at\ 10s\ even\ though\ code)

verdict.summary: Vercel serverless function timeout varies by plan: Hobby=10s, Pro=60s, Enterprise=900s. The Hobby 10s limit is not prominently shown in docs. Database connections, external API calls, and cold starts easily exceed 10s.

dead_ends.action: Optimize function code to run within 10 seconds
dead_ends.why_fails: 10s includes cold start time (1-3s for Node.js, 5-10s for Python/Java). Actual code execution budget is 7-9s. With a database connection, you may have <5s for logic.

dead_ends.action: Use Edge Functions for longer execution time
dead_ends.why_fails: Edge Functions have even lower limits (some regions enforce shorter timeouts) and don't support all Node.js APIs. They're for fast, lightweight transformations.

workarounds.action: Upgrade to Pro plan for 60s timeout if 10s is insufficient
workarounds.how: Vercel Pro: $20/month, 60s function timeout. Often the simplest fix for timeout issues.

workarounds.action: Use background functions (Vercel cron or queued) for long-running tasks
workarounds.how: Use Vercel's maxDuration in vercel.json and Background Functions for tasks >10s

workarounds.action: Move long-running tasks to external services (AWS Lambda, Cloud Functions)
workarounds.how: Trigger external function via HTTP from Vercel edge. External function has its own timeout limits.
